name: Saltimboca Alla Romana
recipeIngredient: 12 slices of veal from the upper leg, cut 1/2 inch thick
recipeInstructions:
1. Place veal slices between sheets of wax paper and pound with the flat side of a meat cleaver to make them slightly thinner than when cut. Season with salt and pepper.
2. Place 2 sage leaves (dried sage is acceptable) and a slice of prosciutto on each veal slice, fold over, secure with toothpicks, and dust with flour.
3. Melt butter in a large frying pan (or two pans with more butter for simultaneous cooking). Cook the veal slices quickly over high heat for about 2 minutes per side until browned.
4. Reduce heat slightly and continue cooking for about 5 minutes, or until the veal is just tender. Do not overcook.
5. Transfer veal to a hot serving platter, remove toothpicks, and keep warm.
6. Deglaze the pan with wine, scraping the bottom and sides with a wooden spoon. Reduce by 1/4 cup and pour over the veal.
7. Makes 6 servings.
From "Great Italian Cooking-La Grande Cucina Internazionale" by Luigi Carnacina, edited by Micheal Sonino, Agradale Press, New York, no date given.
NOTE: Although generally considered a Roman specialty, this dish originated in Brescia.
